title: anacamptis papilionacea subsp. schirwanica
intro: Anacamptis papilionacea subsp. schirwanica, also known as the butterfly orchid, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the Anacamptis papilionacea plant that is native to the Caucasus region. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Caucasus region, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Anacamptis papilionacea subsp. schirwanica include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's tubers and roots are typically dried and used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
